gloss.en: a long, narrow opening to fit something into

gloss.en: to break violently and noisily into pieces

gloss.en: to break suddenly with a sharp noise

gloss.en: to put in liquid so it gets fully wet

gloss.en: to rise very quickly in amount or level

words.example: He holds socialist beliefs.

gloss.en: only; single

gloss.en: only; not involving anyone else

gloss.en: a lawyer who advises and prepares legal documents

gloss.en: support among people who share aims or feelings

gloss.en: done by one person alone, without help

gloss.en: sensible and reliable; likely to give good results

gloss.en: complete power to govern a country

gloss.en: unwanted advertising sent by email

gloss.en: the length of time something lasts

gloss.en: free to use as you want; extra

gloss.en: to cause something to start, especially suddenly

gloss.en: a small sample that shows what the rest is like

gloss.en: a striking or impressive sight or show

gloss.en: a short period of time

gloss.en: a solid round shape like a ball

gloss.en: a very fast turning movement

words.example: We can now measure changes in the earth's spin.

gloss.en: the row of bones down the middle of the back

gloss.en: a bright beam of light aimed at one place

gloss.en: a husband or wife

gloss.en: a person who secretly gathers information

words.example: He was denounced as a foreign spy.

gloss.en: a section of a police force or team

gloss.en: to press something, especially with your fingers

gloss.en: to push a sharp object into someone

words.example: He was stabbed to death in an attack.

gloss.en: the state of being steady and not changing

words.example: The country enjoyed years of political stability.

gloss.en: to become or make steady and unlikely to change

gloss.en: money that someone invests in a company

gloss.en: done from an upright position, not sitting

words.example: He did a standing jump over the bar.

gloss.en: harsh, real, and impossible to avoid

gloss.en: connected with or based on statistics

words.example: They did a careful statistical analysis.

gloss.en: to control the direction a vehicle moves

gloss.en: the long thin part of a plant

gloss.en: a fixed, often untrue idea about a group

gloss.en: something that helps growth or development

gloss.en: to move a liquid around with a spoon to mix it

gloss.en: keeping things in a place until needed; storage space

words.example: These tables fold flat for storage.

gloss.en: easy to do or understand; not complicated

gloss.en: pressure from very heavy demands

gloss.en: a single thin piece of thread, hair, or wire

gloss.en: done as part of a plan to gain an advantage

gloss.en: unusual enough to attract attention

words.example: The building has one striking feature.

gloss.en: a long narrow piece of paper, cloth, or metal

gloss.en: to try very hard to achieve something

gloss.en: connected with how something is built or organized

gloss.en: to hit your foot and almost fall while walking

gloss.en: to make someone briefly unconscious by a blow

gloss.en: accepting defeat and obeying someone

gloss.en: money paid by a government to keep prices low

gloss.en: large in amount, value, or importance

gloss.en: very much; a lot

words.example: The costs have increased substantially.

gloss.en: a person or thing used in place of another

gloss.en: using one person or thing in place of another

gloss.en: not obvious or easy to notice

gloss.en: in or connected with a suburb

gloss.en: a number of people or things following each other

gloss.en: following immediately one after another

words.example: This was their fourth successive win.

gloss.en: a person or thing that takes another's place

gloss.en: to draw liquid or air into your mouth

words.example: She likes to suck the juice from an orange.

gloss.en: to make a legal claim against someone in court

words.example: They threatened to sue if the work was not finished.

gloss.en: the act of killing yourself deliberately

gloss.en: the highest point, especially of a mountain

gloss.en: excellent; of very good quality

gloss.en: better in quality than someone or something else

gloss.en: to be in charge and ensure things are done right

gloss.en: a thing added to improve or complete something

gloss.en: giving help, encouragement, or sympathy

gloss.en: according to what is believed but not certain

gloss.en: to put an end to something, often by force

gloss.en: highest in rank or position

gloss.en: a sudden increase of a strong feeling

gloss.en: used in or connected with surgery

gloss.en: an amount that is more than you need

gloss.en: to admit defeat and stop fighting

gloss.en: close watching of a suspect or place

gloss.en: removing someone from a job or school temporarily

gloss.en: a feeling that someone has done wrong

gloss.en: feeling that someone has done something wrong

gloss.en: to provide enough for something to live or exist

gloss.en: a swinging movement or rhythm

gloss.en: a weapon with a long metal blade and handle

gloss.en: containing or used as a symbol

gloss.en: kind, gentle, and loving

gloss.en: the period of holding an important job

gloss.en: excellent; wonderful

gloss.en: based on ideas and principles rather than practice
